cases.name: der_len_overflow_4byte_max
cases.description: SEQUENCE with 4-byte long-form length = 0xFFFFFFFF. Parser must handle this as > remaining buffer without integer overflow in size calculation.

cases.name: der_len_overflow_8byte
cases.description: SEQUENCE with 8-byte long-form length (0x88). If parser only reads 4 bytes, remaining bytes become content → tag confusion. If it reads all 8, the 64-bit value could truncate to 0x10 in 32-bit comparison.

cases.name: der_len_5byte_overflow
cases.description: 5-byte long-form DER length (0x85). Value = 0x0100000020 = 4GB+32. If truncated to 32-bit → only 0x20 (32 bytes). Parser reads 32 bytes but thinks structure is 4GB → subsequent parsing OOB.

cases.name: der_len_indefinite_form
cases.description: DER indefinite form (0x80). Illegal in DER but parsers may handle it. Could cause unbounded read until 0x00 0x00 end-of-contents marker.

cases.name: der_len_exact_boundary
cases.description: SEQUENCE with length exactly at DFU buffer boundary. Tests parser behavior when claimed structure size = available data.

cases.name: der_children_exceed_parent
cases.description: SEQUENCE claims 0x50 bytes but children total 0x208 bytes. Tests if parser validates sum(child_lengths) <= parent_length.

cases.name: asn1_nesting_depth_50
cases.description: 50 nested SEQUENCE tags. SecureROM stack is limited. Each recursive parser call uses ~0x30-0x60 stack bytes → at depth 50: ~0xc80 bytes of stack consumed.

cases.name: asn1_nesting_depth_100
cases.description: 100 nested SEQUENCE tags. SecureROM stack is limited. Each recursive parser call uses ~0x30-0x60 stack bytes → at depth 100: ~0x1900 bytes of stack consumed.

cases.name: asn1_nesting_depth_200
cases.description: 200 nested SEQUENCE tags. SecureROM stack is limited. Each recursive parser call uses ~0x30-0x60 stack bytes → at depth 200: ~0x3200 bytes of stack consumed.

cases.name: asn1_nesting_depth_400
cases.description: 400 nested SEQUENCE tags. SecureROM stack is limited. Each recursive parser call uses ~0x30-0x60 stack bytes → at depth 400: ~0x6400 bytes of stack consumed.

cases.name: asn1_mixed_nesting_150
cases.description: 150 levels of mixed SEQUENCE/SET/context tags. Each tag type may use different parser dispatch, increasing stack usage.

cases.name: img4_tag_type_confusion
cases.description: IMG4 container with BOOLEAN where IA5STRING 'IMG4' expected. Tests if parser checks tag type before processing value.

cases.name: im4p_payload_type_confusion
cases.description: IM4P with INTEGER (0x02) where OCTET STRING (0x04) expected for payload. If parser reads length without checking tag → type confusion.

cases.name: img4_context_tag_31
cases.description: IMG4 with context tag [31] (long-form tag encoding, 0xBF 0x1F). If dispatch table is indexed by tag number without bounds → OOB!

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x00
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x00 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x01
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x01 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x03
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x03 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x05
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x05 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x06
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x06 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x0c
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x0C at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x13
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x13 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x17
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x17 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x18
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x18 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x1e
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x1E at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x1f
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x1F at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x30
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x30 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x31
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x31 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x40
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x40 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0x80
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0x80 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0xa0
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0xA0 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0xc0
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0xC0 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0xe0
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0xE0 at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_tag_value_0xff
cases.description: IMG4 with tag byte 0xFF at payload position. Tests parser tag dispatch for unusual/reserved tag values.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_1
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 1 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_2
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 2 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_3
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 3 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_4
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 4 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_8
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 8 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_16
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 16 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_32
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 32 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_64
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 64 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_156
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 156 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: img4_truncated_at_312
cases.description: Valid IMG4 container truncated after 312 bytes. Tests every parser path for premature EOF handling.

cases.name: der_tag_only_no_length
cases.description: Bare SEQUENCE tag (0x30) with no length byte. Parser must not read beyond buffer.

cases.name: der_tag_length_no_value
cases.description: SEQUENCE with length=256 but no value bytes. Tests if parser validates available data before reading.

cases.name: im4p_large_payload
cases.description: IM4P with 0x700-byte payload filling most of the 0x800 DFU buffer.

cases.name: im4p_fake_lzfse
cases.description: IM4P with LZFSE magic (bvx2) in payload. If ROM tries to decompress, malformed LZFSE data could cause heap overflow in decompressor.

cases.name: im4p_fake_lzss
cases.description: IM4P with LZSS magic and claimed decompressed size 0x10000. Decompressor heap overflow if output buffer allocated from claimed size.

cases.name: im4p_empty_component
cases.description: IM4P with zero-length component and description strings.

cases.name: im4p_with_kbag
cases.description: IM4P with 5th element (KBAG/keybag). Tests if parser handles extra elements and if AES key unwrap code has exploitable paths.

cases.name: manifest_size_0_bytes
cases.description: Raw 0-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: manifest_size_1_bytes
cases.description: Raw 1-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: manifest_size_15_bytes
cases.description: Raw 15-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: manifest_size_16_bytes
cases.description: Raw 16-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: manifest_size_17_bytes
cases.description: Raw 17-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: manifest_size_32_bytes
cases.description: Raw 32-byte payload sent to manifest path. ROM's check at 0x10000E540: cmp w9, #0xf (>= 16 required). Tests exact boundary condition.

cases.name: full_2048_block
cases.description: Exactly 2048 bytes (one full DFU block). Tests wLength=0x800 path.

cases.name: dfu_size_0x7ff
cases.description: Payload size 0x7FF — testing around the cmp #0x801 check at 0x10000E4AC.

cases.name: dfu_size_0x800
cases.description: Payload size 0x800 — testing around the cmp #0x801 check at 0x10000E4AC.

cases.name: dfu_size_0x801
cases.description: Payload size 0x801 — testing around the cmp #0x801 check at 0x10000E4AC.

cases.name: octet_string_oversize_claim
cases.description: OCTET STRING claims 0x400 bytes but only 0x10 provided. Parser may read 0x400 bytes into heap → OOB read.

cases.name: octet_string_zero_with_trailing
cases.description: OCTET STRING with length=0 followed by hidden data. Tests if parser advances past the 0-length field correctly.

cases.name: der_longform_zero_length
cases.description: SEQUENCE with long-form length encoding for value 0 (0x81 0x00). Should be short-form. Some parsers mishandle this.

cases.name: der_len_signedness
cases.description: SEQUENCE with length 0xFFFF. If cast to signed 16-bit = -1. If extended to 32-bit signed = 0xFFFFFFFF → massive OOB.

cases.name: der_len_0x80_reserved
cases.description: Length byte 0x80 is reserved (indefinite form) in DER. BER allows it. Parser behavior undefined → potential OOB.

cases.name: img4_memz_magic
cases.description: IM4P payload starts with 'Memz' (0x4D656D7A) magic. img4_verify checks for this at 0x10000A7D4. Takes different code path than standard img4 magic → less tested → more likely buggy.

cases.name: img4_deep_inner_der
cases.description: IMG4 with IM4P payload containing nested DER structures. If parser recursively parses payload content, inner structures trigger additional parser code paths.

cases.name: raw_magic_cpid
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x8020 (cpid). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: raw_magic_vid
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x5AC (vid). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: raw_magic_event30k
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x30000 (event30k). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: raw_magic_event40m
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x40030000 (event40m). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: raw_magic_img4
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x696D3470 (img4). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: raw_magic_IM4P_be
cases.description: Raw payload starting with 0x494D3450 (IM4P_be). Tests if parser finds false magic matches in attacker data.

cases.name: img4_full_manifest_trigger
cases.description: Full IMG4 with IM4P + IM4M manifest containing MANP properties. This should trigger the full manifest verification path including property iteration at 0x100004CB8 (dispatch through tag table).

cases.name: img4_child_exceeds_parent
cases.description: IMG4 outer SEQUENCE claims 100 bytes less than actual content. If parser trusts parent length → child parsing reads wrong offsets. If parser trusts child length → reads past parent boundary.

cases.name: img4_double_im4p
cases.description: IMG4 with TWO IM4P elements. Parser may only process first, second, or attempt both → state confusion if structures share buffers.

cases.name: payload_false_tags
cases.description: Raw payload filled with repeating SEQUENCE headers. If parser scans forward looking for structure → matches at wrong positions.

cases.name: payload_all_ff
cases.description: All-0xFF payload. Tag 0xFF = application constructed [31], long form. Stresses tag parsing limits.

cases.name: payload_sram_address_spray
cases.description: Payload containing SRAM addresses (0x19C010Bxx). If parser stores pointers from parsed data, these values could corrupt DFU globals.

usage.dfu_test: Send each .bin via DFU DNLOAD → zero-length DNLOAD → GETSTATUS
usage.crash_indicator: Device disappears from USB = crash (potential vuln)
usage.timing_indicator: Manifest time differs significantly from baseline = different code path
usage.error_indicator: dfuERROR state = parser rejected (expected for most cases)
